doc: Pure-TS port of GMP mpn_copyd: copy n limbs from s in decreasing-address order. Substrate-class -- operates on raw bigint arrays, not src/core.ts MPFR values; runner sets requireCoreImport=false for substrate. In C, mpn_copyd matters when src and dst overlap with dst > src (high addresses first prevents overwriting un-read limbs); in the immutable TS substrate the iteration direction is irrelevant because every return is a fresh array. Kept as a separate export so the GMP I/O contract matches name-for-name with C callers (e.g. mpfr_frac uses mpn_copyd specifically).
c_signature: void mpn_copyd(mp_limb_t *rp, const mp_limb_t *sp, mp_size_t n)
refs: mpfr/src/frac.c L110 -- canonical caller: `mpn_copyd (tp + t0, up, un + 1);` (the fractional-part extraction's limb shuffle).
